tool.name: RNAaliduplex
tool.description: Find binding sites of two RNA alignments

param.label: Compute suboptimal structures within this energy range of the optimum [kcal/mol]

param.name: dangling
param.label: How to treat dangling end energies
param.option: 1: unpaired bases participate in one dangling end only
2: unpaired bases participate in all dangling ends

param.name: noclosinggu
param.label: Allow GU pairing at the ends

param.name: notetra
param.label: Allow stabilization for loops, hairpins etc.

param.name: nsp
param.label: Allow non-standard pairs in addition to AU, GC, and GU pairs.
param.help: Comma-separated list of additionally allowed pairs.

tool.help:
**RNAaliduplex**
Reads two alignments of RNA sequences in CLUSTAL format and predicts optimal and suboptimal binding sites, hybridization energies and the corresponding structures. The calculation takes only inter-molecular base pairs into account; for the general case use RNAcofold. The use of alignments allows focusing on binding sites that are evolutionarily conserved. The two input alignments need to have an equal number of sequences in the same order.
-----
**Input format**
RNAaliduplex takes two alignment files in Clustal format (\*.aln).
-----
**Outputs**
- Text output with secondary structures and hybridization energies - Each line: structure in dot-bracket format with '&' separating strands, ranges in format "from,to : from,to", and energy in kcal/mol
